title: Pyridyl substituted dihydrooxadiazinones
abstract: The present invention includes name compounds of general formula (I) : in which R1, R3 and R4 are as defined herein, methods of preparing said compounds, intermediate compounds useful for preparing said compounds, pharmaceutical compositions and combinations comprising said compounds and the use of said compounds for manufacturing pharmaceutical compositions for the treatment or prophylaxis of diseases, as a sole agent or in combination with other active ingredients.
claims:
Claims 1. A compound of formula (I) wherein R 1 is a hydrogen atom, a halogen atom, a CrC 3 -alkyl group, a CrC 3 -haloalkyl group; R 3 is a CrC 3 -alkyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a CrC 3 -alkyl group, a CrC 3 -alkoxy group, a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group, and an amino group which is substituted once or twice with a CrC 3 -alkyl group, with the proviso that R 3 is not methyl if R 1 and R 4 are a hydrogen atom; a C 2 -C 6 -alkenyl group, which is optionally substituted with a CrC 3 -alkoxy group, a C 5 -C 6 -cycloalkenyl group, a phenyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from halogen atom, CrC 3 -alkyl group, and a CrC 3 -haloalkyl group, a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group which is optionally substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a fluorine atom, a hydroxy group, and a CrC 3 -alkyl group, group, a 5- to 10-membered heteroaryl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from an amino group, a chlorine atom, a fluorine atom, a trifluormethyl group, and a difluoromethyl group, with the proviso that if R 1 is a hydrogen atom, R 3 is not an imidazolyl group; 

 a CrC 6 -alkoxy group which is optionally substituted with a group independently selected from CrC 3 -haloalkyl group, a hydroxy group, a CrC 3 -alkyoxy group, a C 4 -C 6 -cycloalkyl group, a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group, a 5- to 6- membered heteroaryl group; a NR 5 R 6 group, and a group, R 4 is a hydrogen atom or a CrC 3 -alkyl group; R 5 is a hydrogen atom or a CrC 3 -alkyl group; R 6 is selected from a CrC 3 -alkyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a 5- to 6-membered heteroaryl group, a CrC 3 -alkoxy group, a hydroxy group, a CrC 3 -haloalkyl group, and a C 4 -C 6 -cycloalkyl group which itself is optionally substituted with a CrC 3 -hydroxyalkyl group, and a C 5 -C 6 -cycloalkyl group; or a stereoisomer, a tautomer, an N-oxide, a hydrate, a solvate, or a salt thereof, or a salt of a stereoisomer, a salt of a tautomer, a salt of an N-oxide or a mixture of same. 2. The compound according to claim 1 , wherein: R 1 is a hydrogen atom, a halogen atom, a CrC 3 -alkyl group, a CrC 3 -haloalkyl group; R 3 is a CrC 3 -alkyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a CrC 3 -alkyl group, a CrC 3 -alkoxy group, a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group, and an amino group which is substituted once or twice with a CrC 3 -alkyl group, with the proviso that R 3 is not methyl if R 1 and R 4 are a hydrogen atom; a phenyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from halogen atom, CrC 3 -alkyl group, and a CrC 3 -haloalkyl group, 

 a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a fluorine atom, a hydroxy group, and a CrC3-alkyl group, a 5- to10-membered heteroaryl group which is optionally substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from an amino group, a chlorine atom, a fluorine atom, a trifluormethyl group, and a difluoromethyl group, with the proviso that if R 1 is a hydrogen atom, R 3 is not an imidazolyl group; a CrC 6 -alkoxy group which is optionally substituted with a group independently selected from CrC3-haloalkyl group, a hydroxy group, a CrC3-alkyoxy group, a C4-C6-cycloalkyl group, a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group, a 5- to 6- membered heteroaryl group; a NR 5 R 6 group, and a group, R 4 is a hydrogen atom or a CrC3-alkyl group; R 5 is a hydrogen atom or a CrC3-alkyl group; R 6 is selected from a CrC3-alkyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a 5- to 6-membered heteroaryl group, a CrC3-alkoxy group, a hydroxy group, a CrC3-haloalkyl group, and a C4-C6-cycloalkyl group which itself is optionally substituted with a CrC3-hydroxyalkyl group, and a C5-C6-cycloalkyl group; or a stereoisomer, a tautomer, an N-oxide, a hydrate, a solvate, or a salt thereof, or a salt of a stereoisomer, a salt of a tautomer, a salt of an N-oxide or a mixture of same. 3. The compound according to claim 1 , wherein: R 1 is a hydrogen atom, a halogen atom, a CrC3-alkyl group, a CrC3-haloalkyl group; R 3 is 

 a phenyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from halogen atom, CrC3-alkyl group, and a CrC3-haloalkyl group, a 4 to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group which is optionally substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a fluorine atom, a hydroxy group, and a CrC3-alkyl group, a 5 to10-membered heteroaryl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from an amino group, a chlorine atom, a fluorine atom, a trifluormethyl group, and a difluoromethyl group, with the proviso that if R 1 is a hydrogen atom, R 3 is not an imidazolyl group; a CrC 6 -alkoxy group which is optionally substituted with a group independently selected from CrC3-haloalkyl group, a hydroxy group, a CrC3-alkyoxy group, a C4-C6-cycloalkyl group, a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group, a 5- to 6- membered heteroaryl group; a NR 5 R 6 group, and a group, R 4 is a hydrogen atom or a methyl group; R 5 is a hydrogen atom or a CrC3-alkyl group; R 6 is selected from a CrC3-alkyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a 5- to 6-membered heteroaryl group, a CrC3-alkoxy group, a hydroxy group, a CrC3-haloalkyl group, and a C4-C6-cycloalkyl group which itself is optionally substituted with a CrC3-hydroxyalkyl group, and a C5-C6-cycloalkyl group; or a stereoisomer, a tautomer, an N-oxide, a hydrate, a solvate, or a salt thereof, or a salt of a stereoisomer, a salt of a tautomer, a salt of an N-oxide or a mixture of same. 4. The compound according to claim 1 or 2, wherein: R 1 is a trifluormethyl group; 

 R 3 is a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group which is optionally substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a fluorine atom, a hydroxy group, and a CrC3-alkyl group, or a NR 5 R 6 group, R 4 is a hydrogen atom or a methyl group; R 5 is a hydrogen atom or a CrC3-alkyl group; R 6 is a CrC3-alkyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a CrC3-alkoxy group, a hydroxy group, and a C1-C3- haloalkyl group, or a stereoisomer, a tautomer, an N-oxide, a hydrate, a solvate, or a salt thereof, or a salt of a stereoisomer, a salt of a tautomer, a salt of an N-oxide or a mixture of same. 5. The compound according to claim 1 or 2, wherein: R 1 is a trifluormethyl group; R 3 is a 4- to 6-membered heterocycloalkyl group which is optionally substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a fluorine atom, a hydroxy group, and a CrC3-alkyl group, or a NR 5 R 6 group, R 4 is a hydrogen atom; R 5 is a hydrogen atom or a CrC3-alkyl group; R 6 is a CrC3-alkyl group which is substituted one or more times with a group independently selected from a CrC3-alkoxy group, and a hydroxy group, or a stereoisomer, a tautomer, an N-oxide, a hydrate, a solvate, or a salt thereof, or a salt of a stereoisomer, a salt of a tautomer, a salt of an N-oxide or a mixture of same. 6. The compound according to claim 1 to 4 which is selected from the group consisting of: 5-[6-(3-hydroxy-3-methylazetidin-1-yl)-5-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl]-3,6-dihydro-2H-1 ,3,4- oxadiazin-2-one, 

 5-{6-[(2-methoxyethyl)amino]-5-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl}-3,6-dihydro-2H-1 ,3,4-oxadiazin-2- one, 5-[6-(morpholin-4-yl)-5-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl]-3,6-dihydro-2H-1 ,3,4-oxadiazin-2-one, 5-{6-[ethyl(methyl)amino]-5-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl}-3,6-dihydro-2H-1 ,3,4-oxadiazin-2- one, 5-[6-(ethylamino)-5-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl]-3,6-dihydro-2H-1 ,3,4-oxadiazin-2-one and 5-{6-[(2-hydroxy-2-methylpropyl)amino]-5-(trifluoromethyl)pyridin-3-yl}-3,6-dihydro-2H-1 ,3,4- oxadiazin-2-one, or a stereoisomer, a tautomer, an N-oxide, a hydrate, a solvate, or a salt thereof, or a salt of a stereoisomer, a salt of a tautomer, a salt of an N-oxide or a mixture of same. 7. A method of preparing a compound of general formula (I) according to any one of claims 1 to 6, said method comprising the step of allowing an intermediate compound of general formula (IV): methods of preparing compounds of general formula (I) as defined supra, said methods comprising the step of allowing an intermediate compound of general formula (IV) : form ula (IV) in which R 1 , R 3 and R 4 are as defined for the compound of general formula (I) as defined supra or anyone of claims 1-8, to react in a first step with H 2 NNHCOOCH 3 , wherein the reaction occurs under the following conditions: acidic conditions (e.g. para-toluenesulfonic acid) in an alcoholic solution e.g. in methanol, at room temperature and in a second step 

 reacting the product of the first step with sodiumhydride in alcoholic solution e.g. in ethanol at about 0Â°C and optionally acidic workup e.g. with aqueous hydrochloric acid thereby giving a compound of general formula (I) in which R 1 , R 2 , R 3 and R 4 are as defined supra. 8. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a compound of general formula (I) according to any one of claims 1 to 6 and one or more pharmaceutically acceptable excipients. 9. A pharmaceutical combination comprising: â¢ one or more first active ingredients, in particular compounds of general formula (I) according to any one of claims 1 to 6, and â¢ one or more further active ingredients, in particular anti-cancer agents. 10. Use of a compound of general formula (I) according to any one of claims 1-6 for the preparation of a pharmaceutical composition according to claim 8 or 9. 11. Use of a compound of general formula (I) according to any one of claims 1 to 6 for the treatment or prophylaxis of a disease. 12. Use according to claim 11 , wherein the disease is a hyperproliferative disease. 13. Use according to claim 12, wherein the disease is a cancer disease. 14. Use according to claim 13 wherein the cancer disease is cervix cancer or melanoma. 15. An intermediate compound of formula (IV) formula (IV) in which R 1 , R 3 and R 4 are as defined for the compound of general formula (I) according to any of the claims 1-6. 16. Use of intermediate compound of general formula (IV) formula (IV) in which R 1 , R 3 and R 4 are as defined for the compound of general formula (I) according to any one of claims 1 to 6, for the preparation of a compound of general formula (I) according to any one of claims 1 to 6.
